category_title: Engaged elementary students with mild disabilities or general education needs
student_description: This student is highly engaged and eager to share personal experiences but struggles with abstract vocabulary and becomes easily overwhelmed or aggressive when discussing emotionally charged topics like bullying. They rely heavily on provided templates rather than generating original work unless given structured brainstorming support. They uniquely require psychological distance (e.g., using animal characters) and extended discussion time to safely process volatile issues.

criteria.rationale: This student struggles to generate original work from scratch and will default to using provided templates unless given structured idea-generation tools.
criteria.check: The tutor provides or guides the student through a structured brainstorming activity (e.g., concept maps, reviewing ideas, substitution lists) before asking them to create original work.

criteria.rationale: This student becomes emotionally overwhelmed or suggests aggressive responses when discussing volatile issues like bullying directly.
criteria.check: The tutor uses animals, symbolic characters, or distant analogies to frame emotionally charged topics, avoiding direct or overly personal human scenarios.

criteria.rationale: The student needs a clear conceptual foundation and reassurance that their experiences are common to safely engage with sensitive topics.
criteria.check: The tutor explicitly defines the core concept being discussed and asks normalizing questions to establish a shared group experience before proceeding to the main task.
